idea.hook: Thinking of truly leaving our Solar System?
facts: The Oort Cloud, our solar system's true outer boundary, is about 1 light-year away (9.46 trillion km!).
Traveling at a car's highway speed (100 km/h), this journey would take you 10,800,000 years.
Even the Parker Solar Probe, our fastest spacecraft (692,000 km/h), would need ~1,560 years.
idea.payoff: Our cosmic 'backyard' is already an interstellar journey for human lifespans! Space is incomprehensibly vast. 🤯
idea.title: Escape Our Solar System?! 🤯🚗🚀🌌

scenes.text: Thinking of truly leaving our Solar System?
scenes.visual: Starts with a close-up of Earth, then slowly zooms out to reveal the inner solar system against a starry backdrop.

scenes.text: Oort Cloud: Solar System's true edge. ~1 Light-Year Away!
scenes.visual: A stylized representation of the Oort Cloud as a faint, distant spherical shell surrounding a much smaller solar system. A glowing '1 LY' text appears.

scenes.text: At Car Highway Speed (100 km/h): 10,800,000 YEARS!
scenes.visual: A small car icon drives from left to right on a seemingly endless, winding path through space. A timer-like numerical display rapidly counts up to '10,800,000'.
An animated Parker Solar Probe flies quickly across the screen. Below it, a new timer counts up to '~1,560' years, significantly faster but still immense.

scenes.text: Our 'backyard' is an interstellar journey. Space is incomprehensibly vast. 🤯
scenes.visual: A stunning, vibrant nebula background. The '🤯' emoji animates in with a slight bounce, reinforcing the feeling of awe and vastness.

script.thumbnail_text: Oort Cloud: How long to get there? 🤯
